Quick actions

Common transactions, in plain English.

Most things you need to do with council can be done online, without phoning or visiting. Here are the most-used services this month.

Online

Pay your rates.

Quarterly or annual instalments. Direct debit, credit card, or BPAY. Hardship support available — call us if you need to talk through options.

Available 24/7 Pay now →
Online

Report a problem.

Pothole, graffiti, fallen tree, broken streetlight, missed bin collection, illegal dumping. Photos and location help us respond faster.

Average response: 2 days Report now →
Online

Find your bin day.

Type your address and we'll show your weekly collection days for general waste, recycling, and (where available) food waste. Add to your phone calendar.

Updated monthly Check now →
In person + online

Plan a build or renovation.

Most home renovations need a building permit. Some need planning approval as well. Check the residential design code or talk to our planners before you start.

By appointment Start here →
Online

Book a park or hall.

Birthday parties, sports, weddings, community meetings. 18 parks and reserves, 3 community halls, 1 aquatic centre with function rooms.

Up to 12 months ahead Browse venues →
In person

Library membership.

Free for residents. Books, e-books, audiobooks, kids programs, and free wifi at both branches. Sign up at either library with proof of address.

2 branches Library hours →

Can't find what you need?

Call our customer service team on (08) 9000 0000, Monday to Friday 8:30am to 5pm. After-hours emergencies — fallen trees, water leaks, road hazards — call 1800 000 000 anytime.

Contact us

Metro Council WA

Delivering local government services to 85,000 residents across 12 suburbs in inner and middle Perth.

Metro Council WA acknowledges the Whadjuk people of the Noongar nation as the Traditional Custodians of the lands on which we work, and we pay our respects to Elders past and present.
